db_assertions: the SH-2 RC bundle EXTENDS the E14 self-host track (plan §T6): the same clean production-profile install spine came up HARDENED (non-dev master key + closed registration + CA-verified TLS edge + green config-validate/doctor v2) and resolved the ten restart-less SPINE steps with restart_count=0 — journey_digest = hashParts(canonical SelfHostStepIDs) is re-derivable + anchored (anti-fabrication, unchanged from self-host-0.1.0)

PROOF SEAM (plan §6): the local-production-compose seam on Docker Desktop with a self-minted local-CA edge cert. The dedicated cloud-VM clean install with a real domain/cert is the operator leg — named, NOT claimed

OPS-005/007 + SAN-011: an ACTIVE long run (surviving_run_id) was alive across an N->N+1 `palai upgrade` (two REAL builds, same binaries + different -ldflags -X version stamp) and SURVIVED on its PINNED engine to completion (surviving_run_completed); its event stream stayed CONTINUOUS across the control-plane recreate — event_continuity_digest = hashParts(continuity_event_ids) is re-derivable (anti-fabrication)

BOTH rollbacks took effect: the app image rolled back to N with the schema still EXPANDED (app_rollback), and the new-run engine ALIAS pointer rolled back while the active run stayed on its pinned engine (engine_alias_rollback); each DRAINED the active run before recreate rather than silently migrating it (rollback_drained — the T2 MF-3 invariant, LIVE-confirmed by the drain-before-recreate ordering)

the upgrade RECREATES the control-plane by design, so this spine is NOT restart-less (unlike the install spine); the load-bearing invariant is the surviving run + the continuity digest. journey_digest = hashParts(canonical UpgradeStepIDs) is re-derivable + anchored

PROOF SEAM (plan §6): N->N+1 between TWO LOCAL builds (a pinned fork-point worktree build vs the current tree) — there is no prior published release yet, so a published-release-to-release upgrade is the operator leg (§6 leg 4); drain is a single-runner batch (SH-0 topology). Named, NOT claimed

OPS-006: the boot migration chain was INTERRUPTED mid-run (a test-only fault killed the control-plane while applying the 000034 contract tranche) and RESUMED on restart to the correct schema_revisions journal head (000034) with NO data corruption — the pre/post row-checksum matched (row_checksum_match). Idempotent re-run of the completed chain is a no-op

the append-only schema_revisions journal (migration no, checksum, applied_at, applied_by version stamp) carries its own self-re-asserting REVOKE UPDATE,DELETE (the usage_ledger precedent); the per-migration bounded lock (lock_timeout/statement_timeout) is the preflight guard

PROOF SEAM (plan §6): the interruption/resume drill on the local production-compose stack (the T1 fault-injection hook). A large-data background backfill is not exercised here — no live case needs it yet; the first real one MUST use this pattern (T1 honest ceiling). Named, NOT claimed

OPS-003: `helm lint` + `helm template` of deploy/helm/palai render the restricted chart and PASS every policy assert (asserts_digest = hashParts(canonical HelmPolicyAsserts) is re-derivable — a bundle that quietly drops no-cluster-role cannot keep a matching digest): ZERO ClusterRole (no_cluster_role — no ongoing cluster-admin), runAsNonRoot + no privileged, NetworkPolicy default-deny, a PodDisruptionBudget, the migration Job as a pre-install/pre-upgrade hook, and external-PG/S3-only (no in-cluster DB)

render_hash pins the `helm template` output for this chart revision (environment-captured, not re-derivable across helm versions — only its shape is gated); the render/policy asserts are the load-bearing proof (tests/uat/kubernetes/render_assert_test.go)

PROOF SEAM (plan §6): render + policy-assert + kubeconform on the LOCAL toolchain, and a `kind` install smoke (make uat-kind) — kindnet does NOT enforce NetworkPolicy, so ENFORCEMENT on a real CNI'd managed-K8s (EKS/GKE) with PSS-restricted admission is the operator leg (§6 leg 1). Named, NOT claimed

OPS-004: the signed air-gap bundle (OCI image tars by digest + the E14 runner host package + CLI + compose/helm + storage/migrations + a sha256sums root signed with the E14 T5 openssl P-256 detached signature — ONE signing tool) re-verified with NO network (offline_network_none: the verify ran inside `docker run --network none`, so egress is TOPOLOGICALLY impossible, not a trusted log line) and a 1-byte tamper made `sha256sum -c` FAIL (tamper_rejected)

the zero-egress install ran on an `internal: true` Docker network with an in-network fake provider + in-network Git fixture — a REAL run completed with egress impossible by topology; manifest_digest is the signed-root sha256sums digest

PROOF SEAM (plan §6): LOCAL bundle build + --network none offline verify + internal-network registry-mirror install. A real air-gapped facility, the operator trust-root/mirror ceremony, and a real private model server are the operator leg (§6 leg 2); SBOM/provenance generation is E18 (manifest fields defined, deliberately empty). Named, NOT claimed

DR-001 primary (database) loss: under steady per-second marker traffic, the pg container + its volume were DESTROYED, then a scripted recovery (fresh pg + restore from the last `palai backup`) brought the stack back healthy AND run-capable. RPO (1.5s) = last_marker_written_at - last_marker_in_backup_at; RTO (37.25s) = recovered_at - disaster_at — BOTH recomputed from the RAW timestamps by the T6 DrillProof verifier (the SAME dr.ComputeRPO/RTO T5's dr.Verify uses), so a hand-edited value FAILS the gate (the measurement anti-fabrication anchor)

the measured RPO is only the writes since the last backup (taken immediately before the disaster); the PUBLISHED self-host RPO target is <= one backup interval and RTO <= 15 min single-node — NOT inherited from the SaaS product (§55.5)

PROOF SEAM (plan §6): 'primary loss' is container + volume destruction on ONE Docker Desktop host; a real instance/zone loss and a separate-physical-host restore are the operator leg (§6, incl. E14 leg 2), DR-003 regional failover is the SaaS plan. Named, NOT claimed

DR-002 (drill-measured): `palai backup` captured stack A (source_project) into one archive (pg_dump -Fc + object-store volume + per-object sha256 manifest, migration_version 34) and `palai restore` loaded it into a SEPARATE clean stack B (target_project) whose no-clobber empty-target gate refused nothing (target_was_empty); manifest_digest = hashParts(kind, migration 34, org ids, sample response) is re-derivable

the restore was run UNDER the DR drill harness with measurement: RPO (0.75s) + RTO (22.5s to healthy + run-capable) recomputed from raw timestamps by the T6 DrillProof verifier — a fabricated value FAILS (drill-measured deepening of the E14 DR-002 round-trip)
PROOF SEAM (plan §6): two isolated production-compose stacks on ONE Docker Desktop host; restore to a separate PHYSICAL host / cloud VM is the operator leg (E14 leg 2). Named, NOT claimed

DR-004 (drill-measured, detection focus): a byte-flip in an object in the store was DETECTED by the backup manifest's per-file sha256 — a tampered archive is fail-closed at `restore verify` (archive_checksum). This is a DETECTION drill, not a timed recovery, so drill_proof carries no RPO/RTO measure (fail-closed detection is the invariant)
the six restore-verify checks are reported together (the shipped InstallRestoreVerify runs them as one); this case pins the integrity + retrievability quarter
PROOF SEAM (plan §6): the restored stack B on the local-production-compose seam; the cloud-VM restore is the operator leg. Named, NOT claimed

DR-005 (drill-measured, key-recovery + isolation focus): with the master-key file wrong/absent, `restore verify`'s secret canary fails CLOSED; an escrow copy of the key makes it usable (file-key seam). Tenant isolation SURVIVED the restore — every org-bearing table still FORCE'd RLS + its tenant_isolation policy (rls_isolation). A DETECTION drill (fail-closed), so no RPO/RTO measure
the no-clobber empty-target gate (buildExcessQuery over every FORCE-RLS table, boot-seed rows excluded) guards against a restore overwriting provisioned tenant data
PROOF SEAM (plan §6): the file master-key seam on the local stack; a KMS-backed key + lease ceremony (SEC-001/003) is E13-H. Named, NOT claimed

DR-006 (secret canary focus): a stored secret still DECRYPTED under the TARGET stack's master key (secret_decrypt) — the canary against a restore that did not carry the source master key (every secret row intact but undecryptable = a silently-dead install). The CLI's AES-256-GCM open is byte-compatible with the control-plane secret store's seal
honest ceiling: at-rest protection is a single master-key AES-256-GCM envelope; the KMS/lease ceremony is E13-H
PROOF SEAM (plan §6): the restored stack B on the local-production-compose seam; the cloud-VM leg is the operator leg. Named, NOT claimed
